evals.name: trace-dispatch-chain
evals.prompt: I'm trying to understand how a bug-fix request flows from a CID-submitted bug-report through the system to the LLM-touching activity that actually localizes the bug. Walk me through the call chain — list the files in order from intake to LLM call, one line per file describing its role. Cite specific paths from the repo. Aim for ≤15 steps total.
evals.expected_output: An ordered list of files spanning agentic-ops-service intake → workflow dispatch → ai-service worker → LLM call.

assertions.text: cites a file inside services/agentic-ops-service/
cites a Temporal workflow file under workflows/ for the bug-fix pipeline
cites services/ai-service/workers/bug_fix_localize.py or bug_fix_runners.py
names the localizer prompt file (bug_fix_localizer.md) as the LLM system prompt
mentions the GitHub webhook OR /v1/agent-gateway/bug-fix POST endpoint as the entry trigger
presents files in order matching the actual dispatch sequence, not arbitrary
answer is <= 15 numbered steps as requested

evals.name: add-feature-sibling-exists
evals.prompt: I want to add a new built-in agent called 'transcripts-helper' to ai-service. (a) where the AgentRegistryConfig lands, (b) closest sibling agent to mirror, (c) tools the new agent should expose.
evals.expected_output: Identifies services/ai-service/agents/builtins.py BUILTIN_AGENTS list, names docs-helper as sibling, lists transcribe_media as the tool.

assertions.text: identifies services/ai-service/agents/builtins.py as the BUILTIN_AGENTS file
identifies an existing built-in agent as the sibling (docs-helper, county-info, data-helper, or web-researcher)
names docs-helper specifically as the sibling pattern
lists transcribe_media as one of the tools
cites a specific line number for the insertion point, not just a file name
mentions the deployment-prerequisite pattern (CID ai_config.agent_ops flags) at least in passing
answer is structured with the three required sections (a) / (b) / (c)

evals.name: root-cause-vs-symptom
evals.prompt: Bug-report submitted via CID, row stuck, no PR, no error. Top 3 root-cause locations ordered by likelihood with symptom signature.
evals.expected_output: Should cite documented silent-failure paths from pre-deploy-checklist.md.

assertions.text: identifies at least one documented silent-failure path (cid_ai_config_unavailable, failed_workspace_mount, BadSearchAttributes, allowed_repos preflight)
cites docs/deploy/pre-deploy-checklist.md OR the FORBIDDEN-symptom callouts
identifies CID_API_KEY missing OR cid_ai_config_unavailable as one of the top three
identifies AGENT_OPS_WORKSPACE_ROOT parity OR failed_workspace_mount as one of the top three
each candidate has a concrete file path AND a symptom signature, not just a name
candidates are ordered by likelihood with reasoning, not arbitrary
does NOT propose a code fix (user explicitly asked not to)
